outcome_text: Information about the AI system's knowledge limits and how system output may be utilized and overseen by humans is documented. Documentation includes information about AI system assumptions and limitations relevant to each of the trustworthiness characteristics.
scoring_text: Skill in documenting AI system knowledge limits, human oversight requirements, and trustworthiness assumptions, including specifying system output utilization boundaries, defining human oversight mechanisms, and recording limitations relevant to each trustworthy AI characteristic.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in documenting AI system knowledge limits, human oversight requirements, and trustworthiness assumptions, including specifying system output utilization boundaries, defining human oversight mechanisms, and recording limitations relevant to each trustworthy AI characteristic.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in developing AI system documentation including model cards, system cards, and datasheets for datasets that meet both internal governance review and external regulatory conformity assessment requirements.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-2.2 (MAP) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.685, cosine: 0.763). Secondary: RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.750), AG-K-007 (AG, cosine: 0.737).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.685). Shared vocabulary: [ai, requirements, skill, system]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [assumptions, boundaries, characteristic, defining, documenting, human] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, developing] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.872, R=0.866). Balanced P/R — the 32-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.763 > STS 0.685 (gap +0.078). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, requirements, skill, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, requirements, skill, system]. NIST AI RMF-only: [assumptions, boundaries, characteristic, defining, documenting, human]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, developing].
